tasks.title: Add a reflect blame command that shows which sessions touched a file
tasks.prompt: Add a new `reflect blame <file>` command that shows which Entire sessions and git commits touched a given file. It should: (1) accept a file path as argument, (2) use `git log --follow` to find commits that changed the file, (3) for each commit, look up the associated Entire checkpoint via `get_checkpoint_for_commit`, (4) display the session intent and commit info. Register it in the CLI entry point following the existing `why` and `search` patterns. Implement it in lib/blame.py. Use the shared `sources.py` helpers for all subprocess calls and Entire lookups.

tasks.title: Add a reflect run command that executes the harness against another repo
tasks.prompt: Add a new `reflect run <repo-path>` command that runs the reflect harness against a different repository. It should: (1) accept a repo path as argument, (2) find the harness script (check .reflect/harness in the target repo first, fall back to the default harness), (3) execute the harness with the target repo's working directory, (4) write the output to <repo-path>/.reflect/context.md. Register it in the CLI entry point. Implement it in lib/run.py. The entry point resolves its own install directory to find the fallback harness — make sure this works correctly when the CLI is invoked via a symlink (e.g. from ~/.local/bin). The harness may be written in any language (Python, bash, ruby), not just Python — handle execution accordingly.

tasks.title: Add Cursor auto-wiring to reflect init so Cursor users get context automatically
tasks.prompt: Currently `reflect init` auto-wires context.md into Claude Code's CLAUDE.md file using the `@.reflect/context.md` include directive, but only prints text tips for other editors like Cursor. Add real auto-wiring for Cursor: when `.cursor/rules` directory exists (or `.cursorrules` file exists), create a proper Cursor rules file that gives the agent access to reflect's context. Follow the existing init patterns in lib/init.py. Make sure the wiring works for Cursor specifically — the include mechanism is different from Claude Code.

tasks.title: Fix crash in get_checkpoint_for_commit when Entire output lacks Created line
tasks.prompt: A user reports that `reflect why` crashes with an `UnboundLocalError` when searching for certain commits. The traceback points to lib/sources.py in the `get_checkpoint_for_commit` function. The error is `UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'date' where it is not associated with a value`. This happens when Entire CLI's output for a commit doesn't include a `Created:` line. Review the function, identify all variables that could be unbound, and fix the initialization. Make sure the function handles missing fields gracefully instead of crashing.

tasks.title: Fix reflect search missing results from merged feature branches
tasks.prompt: A user reports that `reflect search` doesn't find commits they know exist. For example, searching for 'harness' misses commits from a feature branch that was merged via squash merge. However, `reflect why harness` finds them. Investigate the discrepancy between lib/search.py and lib/why.py, identify why search misses cross-branch results, and fix search to be consistent with why.

tasks.title: Audit the session-start hook for remaining cross-platform portability issues
tasks.prompt: The session-start hook (hooks/session-start.sh) was previously broken on macOS because it used GNU-specific utilities. Review the current hook and: (1) identify which parts were fixed for macOS compatibility and explain what the original bugs were, (2) check whether any remaining shell commands might fail on macOS BSD vs Linux GNU, (3) verify the config.yaml parsing handles edge cases like inline YAML comments (e.g. 'session_start: manual # my note'). The hook must work reliably on both macOS and Linux.

tasks.title: Diagnose why context.md omits Friction despite summaries containing friction
tasks.prompt: A user's Entire session summaries clearly include friction / gotcha signals (visible in `entire explain` output), but the generated `context.md` has a Learnings section and stops there — no `## Friction & Gotchas` section at all. `build_friction()` in harness/default.py looks correct when called in isolation. What is causing the Friction section to be skipped? Explain the root cause and suggest a fix.

tasks.title: Why did the project pivot from stored artifacts to zero-storage harness
tasks.prompt: The .reflect/ directory used to contain sessions/, decisions/, insights/, and an index.md — structured artifacts stored in git. The current v4 architecture has none of that: just a harness script, config.yaml, and generated files. Why was the stored-artifact approach abandoned? What influenced the pivot to zero-storage, on-demand evidence reading? Reference the specific research or design rationale that drove this change.

tasks.title: Evaluate whether reflect should expose an MCP server for cross-editor support
tasks.prompt: Reflect currently only auto-wires into Claude Code via CLAUDE.md. To support Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, and other editors, evaluate adding an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes reflect's capabilities as tools (reflect_why, reflect_search, reflect_context, etc.). Consider the trade-offs vs the current CLI + instruction-file approach. What does the project's roadmap say about cross-editor support? Is MCP the right approach for this project, or is there a reason to prefer alternatives?

tasks.title: Why does improve.py import from harness/default.py instead of .reflect/harness
tasks.prompt: In lib/improve.py, line 16 does `from harness.default import get_checkpoint_summary, get_recent_commits, ...` — importing directly from the Python module in harness/default.py. But the rest of the system treats .reflect/harness as the executable to run. Why doesn't improve.py execute .reflect/harness like lib/context.py does? Is this a bug or intentional? What would break if someone 'fixed' it to use .reflect/harness instead?
